compiled_state.title: validate-issues.sh crashes on int created_at timestamp
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description
validate-issues.sh line 35 crashes with TypeError: 'int' object is not subscriptable when processing tickets whose created_at field is an integer timestamp (epoch seconds) rather than an ISO string.

## Reproduction
Run: .claude/scripts/dso validate-issues.sh --quick --terse

## Root Cause
The inline Python code does ts = created[:19].replace('T', ' ') which assumes created_at is a string. The event-sourced ticket system stores created_at as an integer (Unix epoch).

## Fix
Convert int to string before slicing: if isinstance(created, int): ts = str(datetime.fromtimestamp(created)) else: ts = created[:19].replace('T', ' ')
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/6: Code patterns understood ✓ — bug is on line 274 in check_orphaned_tasks(): ts = created[:19].replace('T', ' ') crashes when created is an int (Unix epoch). The get_shared_issues_json() function passes through created_at as-is (line 182: created = t.get('created_at') or t.get('created') or None), so integers reach the slicing code unchanged.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3/6: Tests written ✓ — test-validate-issues-timestamp.sh has 3 tests. Test 2 is RED: 3 orphaned tasks with int created_at fail to produce MAJOR cluster warning because TypeError is silently swallowed by the except block, causing all int-timestamp tickets to be silently dropped from cluster detection.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 4/6: Implementation complete ✓ — fixed check_orphaned_tasks() cluster detection in validate-issues.sh line ~273: added isinstance(created, int) branch that converts epoch seconds via datetime.fromtimestamp() before slicing, preserving the existing ISO-string path unchanged.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: Tests GREEN ✓ — all 3 tests pass. Test 2 (the RED regression test) now passes: 3 same-hour orphaned tasks with int created_at correctly produce MAJOR cluster warning.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — validate-issues.sh runs cleanly against the real tracker (exit 3 = health score 2/5 due to pre-existing orphaned task warnings, no TypeError or traceback). Fix verified end-to-end.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: int timestamp handling in validate-issues.sh
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
